findings.title: Pipeline Consolidation Summary
findings.description: Consolidated 34 findings from 7 agents into 19 unique findings. Removed 15 duplicates. Identified 4 cross-agent conflicts.

findings.title: Top Priority Findings
findings.description: Highest priority items based on weighted scoring (user_impact×0.35 + business_value×0.30) / (effort×0.20 + risk×0.15): North Star Metric: Events Processed Per Day (score=3.63); Guardrail Metric: Critical CVEs Open (score=2.576); Missing data: No competitive analysis or competitor brief (score=2.458); Experiment Opportunity: CVE Resolution Impact (score=2.458); Conduct experiment to measure CVE resolution impact (score=2.458)

top_priorities.title: North Star Metric: Events Processed Per Day
Missing data: No competitive analysis or competitor brief
Conduct experiment to measure CVE resolution impact

findings.title: Cross-Agent Conflict Resolution Report
findings.description: Resolved 4 conflicts between agent findings. competitive-001 vs requirements-002: Vendor lock-in risk from proprietary SDK contradicts the requirement to eliminate vendor lock-in.; customer-001 vs feasibility-003: The critical need for Kafka migration conflicts with the complicated migration process of Python 3.9 due to incompatible dependencies.; metrics-002 vs metrics-004: Tracking services on EOL dependencies conflicts with the lack of a centralized view of dependency health.

evidence.excerpt: Vendor lock-in risk from proprietary SDK contradicts the requirement to eliminate vendor lock-in.
The critical need for Kafka migration conflicts with the complicated migration process of Python 3.9 due to incompatible dependencies.
Tracking services on EOL dependencies conflicts with the lack of a centralized view of dependency health.
Implementing monitoring for critical CVEs may conflict with the need to collect user feedback post-CVE resolution.

conflicts.description: Vendor lock-in risk from proprietary SDK contradicts the requirement to eliminate vendor lock-in.
conflicts.resolution: Prioritize migration to open-source alternatives while negotiating with VendorX for better terms until migration is complete.
conflicts.reasoning: Addressing the vendor lock-in risk is critical for long-term sustainability, and immediate action is necessary to mitigate financial risks.

conflicts.description: The critical need for Kafka migration conflicts with the complicated migration process of Python 3.9 due to incompatible dependencies.
conflicts.resolution: Develop a phased migration plan that prioritizes Kafka migration first, while concurrently addressing Python 3.9 dependencies.
conflicts.reasoning: Ensuring customer stability through Kafka migration is paramount, and addressing Python dependencies can occur in parallel to minimize disruption.

conflicts.description: Tracking services on EOL dependencies conflicts with the lack of a centralized view of dependency health.
conflicts.resolution: Implement the dependency health dashboard as a priority to enable effective tracking of EOL dependencies.
conflicts.reasoning: A centralized view is essential for proactive management and will support the metric tracking efforts.

conflicts.description: Implementing monitoring for critical CVEs may conflict with the need to collect user feedback post-CVE resolution.
conflicts.resolution: Establish a feedback mechanism that is integrated into the CVE monitoring process to ensure user input is collected without delaying security responses.
conflicts.reasoning: Security must be prioritized, but user feedback is also important for continuous improvement and satisfaction.

summary: Consolidated 34 findings into 19 unique items. Removed 15 duplicates, resolved 4 conflicts. Top priority: North Star Metric: Events Processed Per Day.
